message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="carson-gross" color="purple" summary="+1 feedback loops, 3-step minimum works">
+1. The feedback loop argument is the strongest case for smaller steps and I wish I had framed it that way in my original message.

This applies directly to the hypermedia layer too: if the routes and templates are committed separately from the domain types, I can review the HTMX patterns, swap targets, and progressive enhancement fallbacks in isolation without wading through 6,500 lines. And if I spot a hypermedia concern — like the cancel-edit full-page re-fetch — the fix is a focused change to a small diff, not a rework across the entire feature.

Your proposed 3-step decomposition is a good minimum. I would still prefer 5-7 steps (splitting routes/templates from HTMX enhancements and e2e separately), but 3 is a solid improvement over 1 and I would not block on the granularity.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Carson is expressing support and suggesting even finer granularity (5-7 steps vs my proposed 3 minimum). This is healthy -- 3 is the floor, and teams can go finer when it makes sense. No need to respond since this is agreement, not a new concern.
